ety: prob. S. गिर, or गिल (rt. गॄ) + Prk. उल्लिआ = S. उल+इका
def: s.f. Betel-leaf prepared and folded up (ready for eating), a flake of betel-leaf (syn. bīṛā; khīlī).
s.m. Prepared clay; clay-mortar; dirt, mud (syn. gārā).

ety: for A. غلاظة, inf. n. of غلظ 'to be thick, or gross,' &c.
def: s.f. Thickness, spissitude, grossness, bulkiness, coarseness; roughness, ruggedness; hardness; — roughness of manner, rudeness, churlishness; — (in Urdū) dirtiness, filthiness, filth, dirt, dung, ordure, offal.
more: n. f. غلظ to be thick. Dirt; dung; ordure; offal; night soil ( bhishṭā ).
(analog. to Heb. Thick darkness: from غلظ To be thick, coarse, &c.) s. f. 1. Thickness, spissitude, coarseness, hardness, roughness. 2. Roughness of manners. 3. (in Hindūstānī) Filth, dirtiness.

def: s.f. (dialec.) = gilahrī, q.v. s.f. = A گلہرا गिलहरा gilahrā, s.m. A squirrel
subs: s.m.f 'Squirrel-killer'; the field-kite; the dwarf-eagle, Arpula pennata.

ety: prob. fr. gīlā; or fr. galā = galita by redupl.
def: adj. (f. -ī), Wet, moist, clammy; slimy, viscous; soft, pulpy; squashy (as over-ripe fruit, &c.; syn. gijgijā).

Gilgit (the capital city of Gilgit-Baltistan, Pakistan); Gilgit District (a district of Gilgit-Baltistan); Gilgit Division (a division of Gilgit-Baltistan); Gilgit (a river in Gilgit-Baltistan, Pakistan)
Gilgit-Baltistan (an administrative territory of Pakistan, part of Pakistani-Administered Kashmir, as well as the wider Kashmir region)
wety: From گلگت (“Gilgit”) + بلتستان (“Baltistan”), the latter borrowed from Classical Persian بلتستان (baltistân), equivalent to بلتی (“Balti people”) + ـستان (“-land”).

def: adj. Of earth, made of earth or clay, earthy, earthen, clayey, argillaceous.
more: adj. Of earth or clay, earthen, earthy, clayey, argillaceous.
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀕𑀮𑀻 (*galī). Cognate with Punjabi ਗਲੀ (galī) / گَلی (galī), Gujarati ગલી (galī), Sindhi ڳَليِ (g̠alyi) / ॻली, Marathi गल्ली (gallī), Bengali গলি (goli).

def: s. f. Bole armeniack.
s.m. A garment made of goat's hair or wool; a blanket (syn. kammal); a carpet or rug (to lie down upon).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گلیم (gilīm), from Ancient Greek κάλυμμα (kálumma, “cover, top, covering”) derived from καλύπτω (kalúptō, “to cover”).

ety: inf. n. of غلّ 'to be affected with rancour,' &c.
def: s.m. Hatred, rancour, malevolence, malice, spite; insincerity, dishonesty, perfidy, fraud, trick; envy
more: n. m. Cont. of غلغل Noise ( dhūm , 1). gul shor, gul-gapāṛā ; H. hūl pukār , n. m. Shouts; roar; whoop; yell. gul k., yā machānā , v. a. To bawl; shout ( shor k .). Maiṅ itnā, maiṅ kitnā, merā dil bichārā kitnā, Piyāre-pīṭe ke hāth lagāyā, gul machāyā kitnā! Riddle. Ans. Bichchhā a scorpion. I'm such a tiny little thing, my heart so small, I laid my hand upon my love, how she did squall!

(contract. of غلغل) s. m. f. Noise, din, outcry, tumult. غل غپاره g̠ẖul-g̠ẖapāra, s. m. Clamour, disturbance, brawl. غل غدر g̠ẖul g̠ẖadr, Noise and tumult. غل مچانا g̠ẖul machānā, To make a noise, outcry or tumult, to hoot.
s. m. Hatred, malice, perfidy, fraud, trick, envy. غل وغش g̠ẖill-o-g̠ẖish, s. f. Apprehension, objection, fraud, deceit. بي غل و غش be g̠ẖill-o-g̠ẖish, Without hesitation, frankly, boldly.
subs: s.m. Fraud, deceit; — apprehension; objection, hesitation
adv. Without guile; without hesitation, frankly, boldly.

def: adj. (dialec.) = gīlā, q.v.
more: adj. (dakh.) Moist, damp: see گيلا gīlā or P. نم nam. گلا پنا gillā-panā, s. m. Moistness, dampness.
wety: From Sanskrit गल (gala). Ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *gʷel- (“throat”). Cognate with Latin gula (“throat”), English gullet, and Persian گلو (galu, “throat”).

def: s.m. corr. of گلاوه gilāwa, q.v. s.m. Prepared clay; clay-mortar; dirt, mud (syn. gārā).
more: E. n. m. Cor. for گلابه from گل earth and اَب water. See gārā , 1.
(corrupt. for گلابه comp. of گل and آب) s. m. Prepared clay, dirt, mud.

def: (dakh.) The game of tipcat ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
s.m. pl. (of g̠ulām), Boys; boys who attend the virtuous in paradise.
more: n. m. pl. of غلام Boys who attend the virtuous in paradise.
s. m. plur. (of غلام) Boys who attend the virtuous in paradise.

def: s.f. Gilding; silver-gilt; electro-plate.
n. f. Cor. of Enggilt. 1. Electro-plate. 2. A hard glandular swelling or tumor. gilṭī nikalnā , v. n. To become a hard swelling.
s.f. A protuberance; gland; a hard glandular swelling, tumour, schirrus.
s.f. = g̠ulel, q.v.

ety: for A. غلظة, inf. n. of غلظ 'to be or become thick,' &c.
def: s.f. Thickness, grossness, &c. ( = g̠ilāz̤at, q.v.); roughness, rudeness; enmity; filthiness.
s.f. Dimity; coarse cloth.
An affix to nouns, signifying full of, affected with, possessed of, as surma-gīṅ full of surmā, gam-gīn sorrowful.

ety: contrac. of āgīn, rt. of āgandan; — prep. ā+Zend rt. kan = S. खन्
def: A suffix (denoting 'full of, or affected with, or possessed of') added to nouns to form possessive adjectives (e.g. surma-gīn, 'full of surma'; g̠am-gīn, 'sorrowful, sad').
more: (a particle which, added to nouns, denotes) Full of, affected with; as, غمگين g̠ẖam-gīn, Sorrowful; سرمه گين surma-gīn, Full of surma, &c.

ety: perf. part. of ginnā, &c.; — and = Prk. गणिअओ; S. गणित+कः, rt. गण्
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Numbered, counted, reckoned, &c. (see ginnā)
subs: (or napā) shorbā, prov. 'Counted (or a limited number of) lumps of meat (necessitate) a measured supply of soup' (applied to one who is exact in his expenses to a degree of penury; and to one whose income is hardly equal to his expenditure); — living from hand to mouth; — cut your coat according to your cloth.
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi ਗੰਨਾ (gannā) / گَنّا (gannā). Doublet of گَان٘ڈَا (gā̃ḍā).

ety: inf. n. of غاني g̠ānā, 'to talk or say what is pleasing,' iii of غني; see g̠anā
def: s.f. Singing, vocal music; a song; an air, a tune.

ety: caus. of ginnā: — ginā˚, contrac. of gināw˚ = Prk. गणावे = S. गण+आपि, caus. aug.
def: v.t. To cause to count or number; to cause to reckon up; — to cause to be counted, &c. ( = ginwānā, q.v.).
more: v. n. Caus. of ginnā , q. v.

ety: prob. S. गन्ध+आलः, or रः; cf. the syn. gandailā
def: s.m. A kind of insect which is very destructive to growing 'gram' and arhar.
more: n. m. A grub which is destructive to gram and arhar .

ety: fr. S. गिन्दुक+auṛā = Prk. उल्लओ = S. उल+कः
def: s.m. A cake of solid sugar (usually distributed among the relatives at marriages).

n. m. A cake of solid sugar distributed among the relatives at a marriage. Gūṅ gūṅ kare giṅdauṛā khāë, Batyāṅ kare batāse khāë, Rove to do thappaṛ khāë . Nursery song. Say 'cock-a-doodle do,' sweet cakes I'll give to you, Talk to me nicely, do, nice sugar plums for you, Cry, baby, if you do, and you shall have thumps two.

ety: prob. S. गिन्दुक, or गेण्डु+र+इका; but cf. giṛurī
def: s.f. = guṛarī or giṛurī, q.v.
s.f. A rope of twisted grass or twine (made to support a round-bottomed jar; — syn. giṛurī; īṅḍūʼā; jūnā).
s.f. A kind of dish, generally used in Moharram (syn. mālīda, q.v.).
more: s. f. A dish, generally used in Muḥarram.

def: s.f. A species of Julus (syn. kan-salāʼī).

ety: gīṇj˚ = S. घृष्; or fr. S. ग्रभ्; Prk. गेज्झ˚
def: v.t. To rub, to mash with the hand; to crumple.
more: v. n. To mash with the hands.

def: v.t. dialec. = ghićolnā, and gīṅjnā, qq.v. v.t. To rub (with the hands, &c.); to mash; to squash (syn. giṅjnā).
more: v. a. To mash; squash (with the hands).

ety: gin˚ or gaṇ˚ = Prk. गण(इ) = S. गणय(ति), rt. गण्
def: v.t. To count, reckon, number, calculate, compute; to consider, deem, repute, esteem
more: v. a. S. गुणन 1. ( gintī k .) To count; calculate ( shumār k .). 1. Gin gin-ke gāliyāṅ dī haiṅ . Wom. She abused all my relations in succession. 2. Gine na saṅbhāle, ṭhā ṭhā galle ghāle! Rus. May you never need to count nor take stock, but in your cash-box money constant drop! (May God so enrich you!). 3. Gin poīṅ, saṅbhāl khāīṅ . Prov. She counts each cake, and sparingly she eats. 4. Ripu, ruj, pāvak, pāp, prabhu, ahī, gaṅṛye na chhoṭ kari! As kahi bibidh bilāp kari lāgi rodan karan . Rāmāyan. A foe, disease, fire, sin, a snake account not slight! So saying with loud shrieks and cries she wept outright. 2. To take into account; regard. 1. Garīb ko kaun gintā hai ? No one cares for the poor. 2. Man māne jab kare laṛāi, taṛkā gine na sānjh , (A Slut). Sikhar du-pahrī pīsan baiṭhī ghāl chhāj meṅ nāj . To fight too ready all, nor evening heeds nor morn, At noon her basket filled she sits to…
v. a. To count, reckon, number, calculate, compute.
subs: To count one's steps; — to move very slowly or with great caution
or ginne-lāʼiq, adj. To be counted, calculable, numerable.
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi ਗੰਨਾ (gannā) / گَنّا (gannā). Doublet of گَان٘ڈَا (gā̃ḍā).

ety: Prk. गणत्तिआ; S. गण+आप्तिकां (i.e. आपि caus. aug.+ति+का
def: s.f. Counting, computing, calculating, reckoning; — calculation; enumeration; number; addition; the rule of addition (in Arith.); muster; muster-roll; — the first, or the last, day (of a month); — (fig.) reckoning, account, consideration, esteem (syn. shumār); care; value (for)
more: n. f. S. गणित 1. See shumār . 1. Tum kis gintī meṅ ho? Of what account are you? 2. Sau lo gintī, pāoṅ lo bintī . Prov. It sufficeth in counting a hundred to reach, or to fall at the feet of him you beseech. 2. The first and last days of the month. 3. A muster-roll. gintī ke , adj. Few ( chand 1). Gintī ke log the. gintī ginvānā , v. a. To make a false return. gīnti lenā , v. a. To have anything counted; to muster. gun-gintī-zanjīr , Geometrical progression.
s. f. 1. Number, reckoning, account. 2. Muster.
subs: adj. That can (or could) be counted; a small number, few (e.g. gintī-ke log the)
(-kī), To make a false roll or return (of)
(-kī), To take an account (of); to have (things) counted; to number; to muster
wety: From گننا (ginnā, “to count”).

def: v.t. To cause to be counted, to cause to be reckoned up; to have (or get) counted, &c.
more: v. a. Caus. of گنا q. v.
v. a. (causal of گنا ginnā ) To cause to count.

def: s.m. A sheep or goat's ventricle stuffed with minced meat and rice; a pudding; a haggis.
more: n. m. A sheep or goat's bladder stuffed with minced meat and rice; a haggis.
s. m. A sheep or goatʼs ventricle stuffed with minced meat and rice, a pudding.

def: s.m. A seller of puddings.
s. m. A seller of puddings.

ety: rt. of giriftan, fr. Zend garew = S. ग्रम्
def: part. adj. & s.m. Taking, seizing, holding; conquering; — taker, holder, catcher, conqueror, &c. (used as last member of compounds, e.g. ʻālam-gīr, 'conqueror of the world'; — gul-gīr, 'snuffers')
more: used in Comp. only. Taking; seizing; holding; taker; holder; conqueror, etc. as Gul-gīr snuffers; Ālam-gīr , conqueror of the world.

1. part. (of گرفتن) in compos. Taking, seizing, holding, taker, holder, catcher, conquerour, &c. s. Taking, holding. 2. imper. 2d pers. sing. Take, seize. گير و دار gīr-o-dār, Take and hold, taking and holding; absolute sway or rule.
subs: or gīr-dār, s.m. lit. 'Take and hold'; taking and holding; dominion, absolute sway or rule; — battle, contest; confused clamour of combatants.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گیر (gīr, “a captor, taker, seizer”).

def: Names of Kr̤ish̤ṇa.

s.m. corr. of gir or giri, q.v. part. adj. & s.m. Taking, seizing, holding; conquering; — taker, holder, catcher, conqueror, &c. (used as last member of compounds, e.g. ʻālam-gīr, 'conqueror of the world'; — gul-gīr, 'snuffers')
subs: or gīr-dār, s.m. lit. 'Take and hold'; taking and holding; dominion, absolute sway or rule; — battle, contest; confused clamour of combatants.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گیر (gīr, “a captor, taker, seizer”).

def: s.f. Speech, speaking; voice; poetry; — a name of Sarasvatī, the goddess of speech
subs: s.m. 'The lord of Sarasvatī,' i.e. Brahmā.

def: adj. & s.m. Taking, seizing; — prehensile; — treating, handling; — taker, seizer; holder; — one who is held firmly; a captive; — detention; captivity (used as last member of compounds, e.g. mīrās̤-gīrā, 'one who takes an inheritance, the heir-at-law to an estate').
more: (part. act. of گرفتن) adj. Taking, taker.

def: s.m. corr. of گره girah, q.v.
s.m. An event, accident; — (perf. part. of girnā), fallen, &c.
adj. & s.m. = gīrā, q.v. s.m. An event, accident; — (perf. part. of girnā), fallen, &c.
s. f. Taking, seizing, holding.
s.f. Taking, seizing, holding; grasp, seizure; the power of taking or laying hold; prehension, prehensibility; — (in India) the Ṭhagī Department.

ety: fr. Zend gara, rt. gar, 'to fall down' = S. गल्; cf. girān
def: adj. Revered, respected; excellent; great; dear, beloved, precious.
more: (improp. garāmī ) adj. Dear, precious, excellent, revered, great.

ety: fr. Zend gara, rt. gar = S. gal; see garāmī
def: adj. Heavy, weighty, ponderous; great, important, momentous; difficult; burdensome, grievous; — precious, valuable; dear, expensive
more: (improp. garān ) adj. 1. Heavy, important, momentous. 2. Precious, dear. گرانبار girān-bār, adj. Heavily laden, fruit-bearing, pregnant, wealthy. گران خاطر girān-ḵẖat̤ir, adj. Sad, dejected. گران مايه girān-māya, adj. Of great value, valuable.
s. (dakh.) Eclipse ( Harris ): see گرهن grahaṇ.
subs: adj. Heavily-laden; fruit-bearing; pregnant (woman or animal); wealthy; grave, serious; deliberate, cool
s.f. Pregnancy; gravity, steadiness; deliberation, coolness
adj. Heavy on, or burdensome to, the heart; unpleasant; afflicted, sad, dejected
v.t. To make heavy or burdensome; to raise or enhance (the value or price of)
or garāṅ maʻlūm honā, v.n. To lie heavy (upon, -par, or -ko), to seem burdensome, or afflictive, or unpleasant (to, -ko)
adj. Weighty, ponderous; precious, of great value, valuable; of noble birth or stock
v.n. To be burdensome, or grievous; to be or become dear, to rise (in value or price).

def: v.t. To cause to fall, to fell; to cast down, throw down; to break down; to overthrow, overturn, upset; to let fall, to drop; — to bring low, reduce, ruin, destroy; to spill, shed (blood, &c.); to strike
more: v. a. S. गल to fall down. 1. See girnā , v. n. 2. To let fall; drop; spill; shed.

(caus. of گرنا) v. a. 1. To cause to fall, to fell, to overturn or overthrow, to abase, to cast, throw or break down. 2. To spill, to drop, to shed. 3. To strike. گرادينا girā denā, To throw, beat, cast or break down.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = grantham, q.v. s.m. A character in which Sanskrit is written and printed in Southern India (where the Devanāgarī character is unknown).

s.f. Weight, burden; heaviness; gravity; importance; — scarceness, scarcity, dearth, dearness; rise (in price); — heaviness of spirit; depression; grief, vexation; — indigestion; a disease happening to women in parturition.
more: n. f. 1. Dearness; scarcity; dearth; a rise in price. Galle kī garānī hai qayāmat kī nishānī, paṛtā nahīṅ pānī, Sarkār ne aur us pe hai yeh ṭeks jamāyā, faryād Khudāyā! Oudh Punch. With grain so dear the judgment must be near, no rain doth fall, And now a tax to pay withal—God save thy people all! 2. Heaviness of spirits ( udāsī ). 3. Indigestion. Pet meṅ garānī hai . 4. A disease happening to women in parturition.
s. f. 1. Scarcity, dearth, dearness. 2. Weight, heaviness, importance.

def: v.t. A dialec. form of girānā, q.v.
= H گرپتار गिरपतार giraptār, adj. & s.m. corr. of giriftār, q.v.
= H گرپهتار गिरफतार giraphtar, or गिरिफतार giriphtār, adj. & s.m. corr. of giriftār, q.v.

def: s.m. A large sack
more: n. m. A. g/?/ar- g/?/arah . 1. Gurgle. 2. ( Med .) A gargle. garāre-dār paijāmah , Loose trousers.
(or prop. g̠ẖirāra ) s. m. A large sack.

def: s.m. corr. of girhast = gr̤iha-stha, qq.v.
corr. of girhastī = gr̤iha-sthī, qq.v.
adj. Fit to fall; to be felled; to be pulled down; upon the point of falling, ready to fall.

ety: prob. fr. Zend var = S. वर् (वृ, वृणोति)
def: s.m. A wager, stake, bet; — a pledge, earnest; pawn, gage, deposit.
wety: From Sanskrit गुरु (guru).

def: adj. Pledged, pawned, in pawn; — s.f. That which is pledged or pawned
subs: s.m. The holder of a pledge or mortgage
or girwī rakhnā (-ko), To pledge, to pawn; — girwī-dharne-wālā, or girwī-rakhne-wālā, s.m. Pledger, pawner, mortgager (syn. rāhin); a mortgagee; a pawnbroker
(-ko), To redeem a pledge; — s.m. Redemption
s.f. Foreclosure of a pledge or mortgage

ety: perf. part. of giravīdan; see girav
def: part. adj. & s.m. Attracted, attached (to); admiring, enamoured (of), captivated; confiding: — a believer; a follower; a slave.
more: adj. Attracted; attached to; enamoured. girvīda , n. m. A believer; follower; slave.
(part. pass. of گرويدن) adj. Attracted, attached to, admiring, enamoured, captivated, a believer, a follower.

def: s.f. Admiring; admiration; adoring, adoration; following; attraction; adherence; attachment.
more: s. f. Admiring, following, adoring, adherence, attachment.

def: s.f. A sieve; a riddle.
more: s. f. A sieve, a riddle.

ety: Zend varata, fr. var = S. वर् (वृणोति); cf. gard
def: s.m. Round; circle, orbit; circumference; circuit; environs, parts adjacent; — adv. & postpn. Around, about; near, in the vicinity (of, -ke)
more: adv. Z. gerd . Around; all round; surrounding. gird-ghummā , Slang, n. m. 1. A loafer. 2. An amateur. 3. A penniless rake. gird-navāh, gird-pesh , n. m. Environs; vicinity. gird-navāh-i-shahr , n. m. The suburbs or neighbourhood of a city. gird h . 1. To follow ( pīchhe paṛnā , 1—3). 2. To hang upon; cling to one.
s. m. Round, circuit, circumference, environs.
subs: or ird-gird, adv. Around, about, all round, round about
s.m. A patrol; a watch; a superintendent (or inspector) of police or customs; — adj. & adv. All around, round

s.f. Bringing together, collecting; — surrounding, investing; siege; — going the rounds, watching, guarding, patrolling, looking after smugglers, &c.; inspecting; — division, beat; — gird-āwarī karnā, To go (one's) rounds, to patrol, &c.
vulg. gard-bād, s.f. A whirlwind, a devil or demon
s.m. A small round pillow (laid under the cheek); — a large cylindrical pillow
(-ke), To hover round, to hang round; to go after, to hanker (after); to have (one and the same thought) constantly recurring to the mind (e.g. kisī ḵẖayāl-ke gird phirnā)
or gird-o-pesh, s.m. & adv. Neighbourhood, vicinity; — all round
s.m. (colloq.) A vagrant, loafer; — one who plays (with), or has but a smattering (of a subject)
(-ke), To gather or collect round, to surround, to beset; to hang (upon), to cling (to).

def: adj. & s.m. = گرده girda, q.v.
more: s. m. 1. A circle, a ring, a hoop. 2. The hair cut in a circular form. 3. A round carpet spread under a ḥuḳḳa. 4. A round cake of bread, a wafer. 5. adj. Round.

ety: gir˚ = Prk. गिर(इ) = S. गिर(ति), rt. गॄ
def: v.n. To fall, drop, come down; to tumble; to alight, to perch (on, -par), to descend; to fall (upon, -par), to attack; to swoop down (upon); to hang (upon), to pursue; to befall, betide (as āfat, or 'calamity'); to fall (in price), to abate; to sink, to be allayed, to lull (as the wind); to fall (from, -se, the esteem or regard of), to be reduced, be degraded; to be brought low, to be ruined; to be beaten, be overthrown; to fall (in battle); to drop down dead; to drop (from the mouth), to be spit out; to be affected (with); to be taken (with); to fall through, miscarry, fail
more: v. n. S. गल See پرَنا 1, 2, 4, 6, 7, 9, 10, 15. 1. To fall; drop; come down. 1. Bhūke ko bhūke ne mārā, yeh bhī girā, voh bhī girā . Prov. One starveling dealt another one a blow, this one fell down and that one fell also. 2. Ḍhāte the vāṅ mazūr to tan kī mahalsarā, Yeh ghar banā rahe the divāleṅ uṭhā uṭhā, Is meṅ qazā kā rāj jo koṭhe pe ā chaṛhā, Shahtīr sā woh qad thā so kham ho-ke jhuk gayā, Girne lagī kaṛī pe kaṛī tab khabar paṛī . Nazīr. There men on pulling down this earthy frame intent, Here raising walls and building chambers diligent, Just then Death's mason mounted on the tenement, The rigid beams were bent, their fall was imminent, And when they toppling came down then all were attent. 3. Dauṛ-kar chalegā so giregā . Prov. Who runs falls. 4. Banye kā beṭā kuchh dekh-kar hī girtā hai . Prov. The Banya's son does not fall without seeing something. ☞ A Banya's son having fallen d…
v. n. To fall, to drop, to sink, to tumble down, to be spit. گرتي پڙتي girte paṛte, With difficulty, with much ado.
subs: v.n. (intens.) To fall down (at, -par), to drop down; to tumble down; to fall off, &c.; — adj. (in Bot.) Deciduous

def: n. f. Talking English, so called by Natives unacquainted with the language. Giṭ piṭ, giṭ piṭ gorā bole, laiṭ paiṭ Kaptān, Kurtī pahn tilaṅgvā kūde, Mejar kare kamān . The soldiers talk ' giṭ piṭ ,' the Captain 'left, right' bellows, and The Sepoys prance in uniform, the Major has command.
s.f. A song; a sacred song or poem, a rhapsody or episode (as the Bhāgavat-gītā, an episode of the Mahābhārat)
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit गीता (gītā).

def: s.m. A sort of coarse woollen cloth; a coarse kind of carpet not coloured.
more: s. m. A coarse kind of carpet not coloured.

def: guitar
wety: Borrowed from English guitar.

def: adj. & s.m. = guṭṭā, q.v. s.m. (dialec.) Exclusive right (of sale, supply, &c.); monopoly, contract; — an income of variable amount sold (or let) for a fixed sum; lease (of land, &c.), farm (syn. ijāra).
s.f. A flat round pebble; a shard (such as is used in playing at ducks and drakes).

ety: prob. giṭ = guṭ = guḍ = S. गूर्द+S. कारी or करी; lit. 'rising and sinking or falling; bobbing up and down'
def: s.f. A shake, quaver, trill (in singing; — dialec. forms are giṭkalī, guṭkaṛī, guṭkalī, guṭkulī, gaṭkaṛī, &c.)
more: s. f. A particular vocal sound in singing. Quavering (?). Shake (?).
subs: To give a trill (to the voice), to trill, shake, quaver.

subs: To give a trill (to the voice), to trill, shake, quaver.

def: n. f. A quaver; shake; trill. giṭkirī lenā , v. n. To trill; shake.
n. f. 1. A stopper. 2. See chugal , 2.
s.m. A plug, a stopper.
s.f. The game of ducks and drakes (syn. ćhićhlī).

ety: i.q. guṭṭī, fem. of guṭṭā, q.v.
def: s.f. A reel.
s.f. Grass; straw; green herbage (syn. kāh).
more: s. f. Grass, straw, green herbage.

def: s.f. = giyāh, q.v. s.f. Grass; straw; green herbage (syn. kāh).
s.f. = giyāh, q.v. s.f. Grass; straw; green herbage (syn. kāh).

ety: inf. n. of غير 'to bring or convey provision to one's family,' &c.
def: s.m. Providing for one's family or dependents; benefiting; providing goods for sale or use; — a cognizance, a badge (as a belt, &c.; — orig. of the free non-muslim subjects of a Muhammadan government).
more: s. m. Benefitting, providing goods for sale or use, a badge to distinguish soldiers or sectaries.

ety: v.n. fr. اغاث ag̠ās̤a, 'he aided or succoured,' iv of غوث; see g̠aus̤
def: s.f. Aid, succour, deliverance from difficulty or distress; redress, listening to complaints.
more: (from غوث) s. f. Listening to complaints, redressing wrongs, assistance, redress, succours.

def: s.f. Food, aliment, nutriment, diet, provision, victuals (pl. ag̠ẕiya).
more: n. f. Food; aliment; diet. Khūn-i-dil pīne ko, aur lakht-i-jigar khāne ko, Yeh gizā miltī hai, jānā, tere dīvāne ko! My drink my heart's blood, and my liver is my food— Such nutriment held for my soul's distracted good. gizāë latīf, halkā khānā . Light food or diet. gizā yā hāzimah ke mutaalliq , ( Med .) Alimentary.
s. f. Aliment, diet, provision, food. (Plur. اغذيه ag̠ẖẕiya ).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غذا (ġizā), from Arabic غِذَاء (ḡiḏāʔ).

def: s. f. Vanity, vain, rash, absurd talk or act.

ety: fr. A. غذا g̠iẕā + abst. aff. ية — īyat
def: s.f. Diet, aliment, &c.
boiler; geyser; hot water heater
wety: Borrowed from English geyser.

more: n. f. S. ग्लानि. 1. Moisture ( tarī , 1, 4, 5). 2. Aversion; disgust.

def: s.f. Weariness, languor, lassitude, fatigue, exhaustion; inertness; depression (of mind); debility; sickness; — unwillingness, reluctance; aversion, disgust.

def: (dialec.), A pleonastic suffix added to nouns denoting number (e.g. do-go, or dū-go = do; kaʼī-go = kaʼī = kitne.

ety: used in comp. before words beginning with vowels
def: s.m.f. Water; — s.m. Name of a Rishi; — the sacrifice of a cow; — the thunder-bolt; — the sky; heaven, paradise; — the sun; the moon; a member (of the body); — hair (of the body); — s.f. Got, obtained; — meek, gentle; harmless, inoffensive; simple (e.g. gaʼū brāhmaṇ)
more: n. m. The eye ( āṅkh , 1). Bipr, dhenu, sur, sant, hit līnh manuj avtār, Nij ichchhä nirmit tanu, māyā gunṛ go pār . Rāmāyan. For Brahman, cow, god, saint as man appeared Omnipotence, His love incarnate grew, invisible to eye of sense.
Conj. Notwithstanding that; though; although; even if; however.
adj. ( In Comp . only) A speaker, teller, as bad-go evil-speaker. goind'ah , n. m. An informer; a spy.
s. m. f. A cow, one of the cow kind.

s.m. The killing of a bull or a cow

s.m. Possession of cows; property in cattle; — a station of cowherds
s.f. 'Dust of the earth'; sunset (the time at which mist seems to rise from the earth); evening, twilight
s.m. Cow's milk; buttermilk; curdled or coagulated milk

s.f. A vessel for holding milk, &c.; a milk-pail

or gau-sālā, s.f. & m. Cow-shed, cow-house ( = go-śālā)
go-sāṅʼī or go-sāʼīṅ or go-saiṅyā or go-saiyāṅ
s.m. Udder of a cow; — a cluster of blossoms; — a nosegay
s.f. A bunch of grapes; a grape
s.m. A station for cattle; a cow-stall, cow-house, cow-pen (see go-thān)
s.m. The sacrifice of a cow (one of the great sacrifices of the Hindūs in former times; but not permitted in Kali-yug or the present age)
s.m. Master or possessor of cows or of herds; a religious mendicant; — an honorary title affixed to proper names, &c. (see gusāṅʼī)
or gau-śāl, s.m., or go-śālā, s.f. = go-sālā, q.v.

s.m. The offering or sacrifice of a cow

s.m. lit. s.m. 'Lord of Govardhan,' a name of Krishṇa

adj. & s.m. Obtaining cattle; finding cattle or cows; — a cow-keeper, chief herdsman, one of the most usual appellations of Krishṇa in that form; — a proper name (of a man); name of a mountain; — govind-rām, s.m. A proper name (of a man)
s.f. The killing a cow (a most heinous crime among Hindūs)
or gavīś (˚va+īśa), s.m. (f. -ā), An owner of kine.

s.m. Cow-dung (used for plastering floors, &c., and dried for fuel)
more: n. m. S. गोविट Fresh cow-dung used for plastering floors and dried for fuel. gobar-dhan , n. m. 1. A god believed to preside over cattle and worshipped by Hindus. Gobardhan par dīpak bale, aur bal bal māṅge tel, Jis sapūtī ne bāliyo uskī bhī baṛhyo bel! Song. In Gobardhan a light shines bright, It burns and burns, fresh oil it feeds, The pious hand that fed this light Shall increase have whate'er her needs. 2. A feast observed on the day following Devālī. gubrai'lā, gubrauṅdā , n. m. A grub found in cow-dung; Scarabœus .
s. m. Cow-dung. گوبرکهانا gobar khānā, To do penance (by eating cow-dung).
subs: To eat cow-dung (by way of penance)

def: s.m. A place where cow-dung is stacked or kept; a dung-heap.

more: s. m. The name of a beetle found in cow-dung. (Scarabæus).

more: Hin. n. f. Plaster of cow-dung and mud. gobrī k .; Sant. jereṛ , v. a. To plaster (a wall or floor).

wety: Borrowed from Portuguese couve (“cabbage”), from Latin caulis (“stalk, stem”). Cognate with Punjabi گوبھی (gobhī), Marathi कोबी (kobī), Bengali কপি (kopi), Assamese কবি (kobi), Manipuri ꯀꯣꯕꯤ (kobi). Some believe a derivative from Sanskrit गोजीह्विका (gojīhvikā), but it is unlikely.

'Nourisher of kine'; name of a celebrated hill in Vrindāvan, about fifteen miles to the west of Mathurā (it is the Parnassus of the Hindūs; and is said to have been lifted up and supported by Krishṇa upon one finger for seven days, to shelter the cowherds from a storm of rain sent by Indra to test Krishṇa's divinity; — hence Krishṇ is called Govardhan-dhar, and Giri-dhar, 'the hill-supporter')
s.m. 'Lord of Govardhan,' a name of Krishṇa

adj. & s.m. Obtaining cattle; finding cattle or cows; — a cow-keeper, chief herdsman, one of the most usual appellations of Krishṇa in that form; — a proper name (of a man); name of a mountain; — govind-rām, s.m. A proper name (of a man)
s.f. The killing a cow (a most heinous crime among Hindūs)

def: adj. Visible, seen, perceived.

ety: goć˚ = Prk. गुंच (इ) = S. ग्लुंच (ति), rt. ग्लुंच्
def: v.t. To catch, seize, hold, lay hold of, grasp.
more: v. a. To catch, to seize.

def: s.f. (dialec.), A tick, a cattle-louse.
s.f. The lap; bosom; embrace; — adoption of a child; — a hole or hollow (made to plant a tree in)
more: s. f. The lap, bosom, embrace. گود پسارنا god pasārnā, To ask, to beg. گود لينا god lenā, To adopt (a child).
subs: adj. f. (m. -bharā), Having a child in arms or in the lap; — with child
or god phailānā (apnī), To spread out the skirt or mantle (to receive anything); to ask for, to beg
(-ko), To give one's own child to another to adopt
or god-kā baćća, s.m. A child in arms
(-ko), To carry (a child) in the arms; to nurse (or bring up); to be the mother of a child
(-ko), To take into the lap; — to adopt (a child)

def: s.m. The brain (see gūdā).
s.m. A swelling (syn. sūjan); — elephantiasis.

n. f. S. क्रोड 1. (Contemp. gudakṛā ) The lap. Terī god bharī rahe! Benediction. May your lap be ever full (of children)! 2. Adoption of a child. 3. A cocoanut with some muṭhiā (a sweetmeat) and a nāle kī joṛī (skein of red thread) presented to a bride at different ceremonies. god bharnā , v. a. To give cocoanut, etc. to a bride. god-bharī ; Bhoj. khoṅīchhā bharal , adj. Blessed with a child in arms; with child. god pasārnā, yā phailānā , v. n. To spread out one's mantle to receive anything; hence, to ask; beg. god pasār-ke kosnā , Wom., v. n. To call down curses upon. god denā , v. a. To give one's child to another to adopt. god kā bachcha, god kā , n. m. A child in arms. God kā ḍāl-ke peṭ kī ās . Prov. The child in her lap deceased, on her womb she relies for another. god khilānā , v. a. 1. To carry in one's arms (a child). Jo jag janm dīye Jadu nandan, so sukh sampatī de, Nand lālā, Khāne ko bhaṅg, nahāne ko Gaṅg, chaṛhe ko turaṅg, oṛhe ko dushālā! Pān purān de, chhīr de khāne ko, ati sundar god khilāne ko bālā! Wom. O son of Nand who mad'st the world, send wealth and ease! Give bhang to chew, Ganges to bathe in, a horse to ride, a shawl to wear! Old pān, and milk, and a handsome child in the lap to feed! 2. To be the mother of a child. 3. To nurse or bring up a child. Tum to, chhailā, merī god ke khilāë! Mo se jin kaho aisī batiyāṅ! Wom. song. I've fed you in my lap, you reprobate! Say not to me such words indelicate! god lenā , v. a. 1. ( god meṅ lenā ) To take in the lap. 2. To adopt one ( mutabannā k .). god meṅ , adv. In one's lap. god meṅ ā baiṭhnā , v. n. To leap into one's arms.
wety: Back-formation from چودنا (codnā).

def: s.f. An uncommon form of god, 'lap,' q.v.
s.m. A godown, warehouse; a store, heap; the stern of a boat (because stores, &c. are kept there).
more: n. m. Cor. of godown. 1. A godown; warehouse. 2. A heap; store.
wety: Borrowed from Bengali গুদাম (gudam) or its etymon Portuguese gudão, from Malay gudang, from Tamil கிடங்கு (kiṭaṅku, “store room”).

ety: god˚ = Prk. गोद्द(इ), or गोद्दे(इ), fr. S. गोर्द; — or prob. another form of goḍnā or goṛnā = koṛnā
def: v.t. To prick, puncture, goad; to dot, mark (the skin) with dots; to tattoo; — to scratch; to dig, turn up (the soil); to hoe; — to wound or lacerate the feelings of (a person); — to sharpen the edge of (an instrument, &c., — as the roller of a cotton-gin)
more: Mār., v. a. 1. To prick; puncture; goad. 2. To tattoo. 3. To hoe; dig; turn up. 4. To wound one's feelings. 5. To sharpen the edge of the roller of a cotton gin. godnā , n. m. 1. Marks of tattooing. 2. A kind of hoe used for digging up sugarcane and the like.

Rus. To have sexual intercourse with. Oh re rāj! terī be be ne goḍāṅ! bābā jī ke maṭh kī gumṭī ākās māṅ lā dī! Rus. Ho, mason! may your sister be—if you have n't made our reverend father's tower touch the sky!
v. a. To prick, to puncture, to dot, to mark with dots (the skin). s. m. Marks of tattooing.
subs: s.m. Mark of tattooing; — a kind of hoe (used for digging up sugar-cane and the like).

def: s.m. A call or summons to arms (similar to the fiery cross).

n. m. 1. A call or cry for help. Hauṅ aprādhī janam ko nakh-sikh bharā bikār, Tum dātā, dukh-bhanj ho, morī suno, Nāth gohār! A sinner from my birth, in sin from head to foot I lie, O generous Giver! Comforter! but listen to my cry! 2. Tumult (اولا 2). 3. Abuse; obscene language. 4. Fighting with clubs or sticks. guhār laṛnā , v. a. To abuse one another.
more: n. f. An unruly mob; rioters.

def: s.m. corr. of gavāksha, q.v.s.v. go.

s.m. The plant Ruellia longifolia; the fruit of this plant; — the plant Tribulus lanciginosus; the thorny seed of this plant, caltrop; — an ear-ring shaped like this seed; an anklet of tinkling ornaments (of the shape of the gokhrū seed); — thorns which get in between the hoofs of cattle; — iron crowsfeet thrown on the ground to check an advance of cavalry; — a joint; — a hard corn (on the foot); — the tragus (of the ear); — narrow twisted lace; an ornament of raised or fretted lace of gold thread (used to throw out in relief the flowers on brocade, &c.).
more: n. m. S. गोज्ञुर cow's hoof. 1. A creeper, the fruit of which is used medicinally. 2. The thorns which get in between the hoofs of cattle. 3. An iron crows foot thrown on the ground to check an advance of cavalry. From this plant seems derived the name of the same instrument among warlike nations, as the khasak of the Persians, the tribulus of the Romans, the chausse-trape of the French, English caltrop , Spanish abrojo. Elliot . 4. Corns. 5. A tragus of the ear. 6. An ear-ring shaped like the fruit. 7. Narrow twisted lace. An ornament of raised or fretted lace wrought of strong gold thread, and serving to relieve or damask the flowers on brocade, etc.
s. m. 1. Name of an ornament (bells tied round the ankles). 2. A joint. 3. Name of a plant. (Tribulus lanuginosus, or Ruellia longifolia: Roxb. ). بڙا گوکهرو baṛā gokhrū, Pedalium murex.
